course_description:
This pharmaceutical science course focuses on the study and application of research in pharmaceutical drug delivery technologies and how it can be applied to clinical context. The objectives are to educate students in research-based strategies for drug formulation development and enable them to integrate their understanding of quality medicine use, drug discovery, and dosage form design concepts.

This course, centred on an inquiry-based curriculum, immerses students in independent research to tackle clinical cases through formulation approaches. Working collaboratively in groups, and individually students will engage in hands-on laboratory work over a three-week period (6 h per week), applying their theoretical knowledge to develop hypotheses, and to develop and optimise pharmaceutical formulations. This course aims to enhance critical thinking, problem-solving skills, and practical expertise, preparing students for the complexities of pharmaceutical research and development to be applied in clinical scenarios such as compounding pharmacy, hospitals, R&D, and community pharmacy. Through this immersive experience, students will gain invaluable insights into the intricacies of drug formulation and its clinical applications.

prerequisites: We recommend completing the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
In each of weeks 1, 2 & 13 participate in a 2-hour workshop face to face. Students will attend one 1-hour workshop focusing on practical tips for your laboratory research project.
Students will be scheduled into a 3-week block of 6 hours per week to conduct group-based laboratory research projects. Please note that additional preparation before each practical with your groups is expected to maximise the value of your time in the laboratory.
course_aims: Pharmacists need to grasp the physicochemical principles of drug formulation and delivery to compound medicines, choose suitable dosage forms, and predict drug effectiveness. This course heavily emphasizes research, active learning, data analysis, and interpretation. Students will conduct a research project in teams, simulating clinical scenarios, and develop a prototype formulation, assessing its validity using variety of quality control tests.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate and apply principles of drug delivery to hypothesise, propose and justify optimum solutions in developing new formulation
Integrate and apply Good Laboratory Practices (GLP) to range of formulation concepts in groups
Integrate evidence from variety of sources and hypothesise a potential solution using the pharmaceutical sciences to solve a clinical problem
Create and evaluate a pharmaceutical product using range of appropriate formulation and analytical methods with a view to optimise the properties of the product
Justify the formulation design based on literature and experimental data via written and oral modes of communication

assessment_details.title: Quiz
assessment_details.due_date: 18/08/2025 5:00 pm Complete the quiz at any time during the 10 days prior to the due date.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The online open-book quiz will assess your learning in week 1 and 2. The quiz will be available for a 10-day period and can be taken at any time during that window. The quiz duration is approximately 30 minutes. The format may be a mixture of question types, for example, multiple choice, multiple answer, matching, ordering, fill in the blank/s, jumbled sentence/s, either/or (e.g., true/false), short answer, and short essay style questions. Questions may be provided randomly from a pool, therefore each student may receive a different but equivalent quiz. This is an online open book quiz undertaken in the venue of your choice. You are required to undertake the quiz without any form of communication with other students. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The quiz will be completed at the link in course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Product creation - 3D printed tablet, suspension or capsule
assessment_details.due_date: 22/09/2025 - 13/10/2025 Submit your final product by the Friday of the third week of your scheduled 3-week practical block. Ideally end of your last practical.
assessment_details.task_description: Working in a team, you will be assigned to formulate and prepare one of the following three dosage forms for a medication: 3D-printed tablets, a suspension, or a capsule formulation. Your team will then conduct pharmaceutical assays as applicable to the dosage form (e.g. uniformity of weight, disintegration, dissolution, viscosity). See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for more details and the marking criteria. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit the final formulation with compounding worksheet and compounding label. Based on the scenario and research outcome there may be more than one product and respective label/compounding worksheet per group.

assessment_details.title: Video/Oral Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: 27/10/2025 3:00 pm This is a group assignment. So organise your time with your group well in advance. Start early.
assessment_details.task_description: Record a video report with your team in which you describe the methods that you used and the prototype that you created. This is a group assessment item. The video report will be in the form of a pre-recorded video presentation using PowerPoint slides (or equivalent). Submit both the video and a pdf of the slides. All members of the team must contribute to the preparation of the slides and filming of the video. Your camera must be turned on and your face visible during the entire presentation video. Any parts of the presentation that do not comply with this requirement will receive a mark of zero. All members of the team who contributed and are visible in the presentation will receive the same mark for this assessment. See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for more details and the marking criteria. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit your video file and pdf of the slides via the link provided in the PHRM3202 Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 2
learning_activities.topic: Pharmaceutical research topics: UQ Extend Active online learning content is provided in UQ Extend. Week 1 - Pharmaceutical research and personalised medicine Week 2 - Delivery of Biologics Learning outcomes: L01

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 2
learning_activities.topic: Pharmaceutical research topics: Workshops Attend a 2-hour workshop each week to discuss and practice quiz questions: Week 1 - Pharmaceutical research and personalised medicine Week 2 - Delivery of Biologics Learning outcomes: L01, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 5 (11 Aug - 31 Aug)
learning_activities.topic: Laboratory research Attend this workshop ONCE. It is repeated weekly during weeks 3 - 5 to ensure everyone has the opportunity to attend. The workshop provides a practical guide to conducting the research project in the laboratory. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 11 (11 Aug - 19 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Laboratory research Attend a 3-week block of 6 hours per week in the laboratory. This is a practical research activity to develop a new formulation based on their clinical scenario. As your time in the lab is limited, expect to also spend time out of the lab before and during the 3-week block planning the work that you will conduct when in the lab. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Final Q&A Closing 2-hour workshop to answer any questions and discuss the laboratory project outcomes. Learning outcomes: L01, L05

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Occupational Health and Safety in the Laboratory Guideline Learning activities
